articles.title: Odisha Govt Orders Doorstep Pension Delivery Under Madhu Babu Pension Yojana Amid Heatwave - Kanak News Odisha
articles.full_text:
ବୃଦ୍ଧ ଓ ଅସହାୟଙ୍କୁ ଘରେ ମିଳିବ ଭତ୍ତା । ପ୍ରବଳ ଗ୍ରୀଷ୍ମରେ ଭତ୍ତା ବଣ୍ଟନ ନେଇ ସରକାରଙ୍କ ଜରୁରୀ ନିର୍ଦ୍ଦେଶ।
In a major relief for elderly and vulnerable citizens, the Odisha government has issued an urgent directive to ensure doorstep delivery of pensions during the ongoing heatwave.
Under the Madhu Babu Pension Yojana, beneficiaries will now receive their pensions at home to avoid exposure to extreme summer conditions.
The directive has been issued by Suresh Kumar Vashisth, Principal Secretary of the Social Security and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Department, to all district collectors.
This move aims to protect senior citizens, differently-abled individuals, and other vulnerable groups from the harsh heat while ensuring timely financial support.

articles.title: Odisha advances summer holidays amid ongoing heatwave - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Odisha has advanced the summer vacations and ordered the closure of schools as a precaution against the ongoing heatwave, Minister Suresh Pujari announced. “In view of the heat wave, the schools have been closed… The summer vacations have been announced in advance… We will ensure zero casualties and fight the heat wave,” he told reporters.
Earlier, on April 24, Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi declared early summer holidays in all schools across the state due to the prevailing high temperatures. The directive applies to government, government-aided, and private institutions, which will remain closed from April 27. However, pre-scheduled examinations, census work, and official duties will continue as planned. The Chief Minister approved the proposal from the School and Mass Education Department.
In a post on X, the state’s Information and Public Relations Department stated, “Chief Minister Shri Mohan Charan Majhi has announced an early summer vacation keeping in view the severe heatwave currently sweeping through the state. Keeping the safety of children in mind, the Chief Minister has taken this decision. The Chief Minister has approved the related proposal from the School and Mass Education Department. According to this decision, all government, government-aided, and private schools in the state will remain closed from April 27. However, previously scheduled examinations, census work, and other office tasks will continue as before.”
The heatwave continues to affect many regions of India. On April 23, a 37-year-old man named Sunil Kumar from Pallippoyil in Kannur died following sunstroke while digging a well at his residence. Despite initial improvements after receiving treatment, he passed away early Thursday morning.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued warnings for multiple regions experiencing temperatures between 406C and 456C. Several parts of Odisha, Telangana, Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh are among those affected. Specifically, high temperatures were recorded in Vidarbha, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, and Uttar Pradesh.
The IMD noted similar heatwave conditions at isolated locations in interior Odisha, Rayalaseema, Jharkhand, Rajasthan, and Gujarat. Most other parts of the country saw temperatures between 366C and 406C, except western Himalayan areas and Northeast India, where readings were below 366C.
Additionally, temperatures were markedly above normal in parts of Himachal Pradesh, Bihar, Uttarakhand, and Chhattisgarh; appreciably above normal in Jharkhand, East Uttar Pradesh, Delhi-NCR, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir, Madhya Pradesh, and Kerala; and above normal in Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Karnataka, and other regions.
The IMD reported near-normal temperatures over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Gujarat, coastal Karnataka, and Lakshadweep.
